                | select_from<User, "u1">("username"_t1.as<"user1_name">(), "username"_t4.as<"user2_name">())
                | inner_join<"f1">(Friendship{}, "u1"_t1.user_id == "f1"_t2.user_id1)
                | inner_join<"f2">(Friendship{}, "f1"_t2.user_id2 == "f2"_t3.user_id1)
                | inner_join<"u2">(User{}, "f2"_t3.user_id2 == "u2"_t4.user_id)
                | left_join<"f3">(Friendship{}, "f3"_t5.user_id1 == "u1"_t1.user_id && "f3"_t5.user_id2 == "u2"_t4.user_id)
                | where("u1"_t1.user_id < "u2"_t4.user_id && "f3"_t5.user_id1.is_not_null());

            // Execute the query and return the results
            return query(conn).value();
        }

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <algorithm>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    // Handle error? But the function signature does not allow returning an error.
    // We'll assume the connection is successful for now.
    // In a real scenario, we would throw an exception or return an empty vector.
    return {};
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Read the entire UserProfile table
  auto users_result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn);
  if (!users_result) {
    return {};
  }
  auto users = *users_result;

  // Process each user
  std::vector<ValidatedProfile> validated_profiles;
  for (const auto& user : users) {
    // Trim the name and email
    auto trimmed_name = user.name;
    auto trimmed_email = user.email;
    // Remove leading and trailing spaces
    trimmed_name.erase(trimmed_name.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_name.begin(), trimmed_name.end(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }));
    trimmed_name.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_name.rbegin(), trimmed_name.rend(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }).base(), trimmed_name.end());
    trimmed_email.erase(trimmed_email.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_email.begin(), trimmed_email.end(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }));
    trimmed_email.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_email.rbegin(), trimmed_email.rend(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }).base(), trimmed_email.end());

    // Compute lengths
    int name_length = trimmed_name.length();
    int email_length = trimmed_email.length();

    // Create the validated profile
    ValidatedProfile profile;
    profile.user_id = user.user_id;
    profile.trimmed_name = trimmed_name;
    profile.trimmed_email = trimmed_email;
    profile.name_length = name_length;
    profile.email_length = email_length;

    validated_profiles.push_back(profile);
  }

  return validated_profiles;
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```

This solution uses the `read` API to read the table, but does not use the sqlgen `trim` and `length` functions. It uses standard C++ string processing.
